title: Tragacantha canescens
taxonomy:
I can find information on Tragacantha canescens.

 Tragacantha canescens  is classified as a species of flowering plant in the legume family Fabaceae, native to the Mediterranean region. The classification of Tragacantha canescens is also known as Astragalus canescens in some botanical systems. It belongs to the genus Astragalus and is further classified as a member of the subgenus Astragalus. The plant's classification reflects its characteristics and relationships with other species within the Fabaceae family. Tragacantha canescens is often studied for its unique features and adaptations to its native environment.
uses: Tragacantha canescens, also known as Hairy Bur Clover, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various purposes. Its roots and leaves are said to possess anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. It is also believed to have antiseptic and antibacterial properties, which can help prevent infections. Additionally, Tragacantha canescens is thought to have antioxidant properties, which may help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. More research is needed to fully understand its medicinal uses and potential benefits.
